data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on Zimmer Biomet, Keeps $109 Price Target Amid Ongoing Multi-Year Transformation
data.description:
Mathew Blackman, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on Zimmer Biomet Holdings. The associated price target remains the same with $109.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mathew Blackman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Zimmer Biomet’s multi‑year transformation and early execution against its 2026 objectives. He views 2026 as a rebuilding year focused on setting achievable targets, restoring management credibility, and executing consistently, and notes that the company has already modestly exceeded expectations across key metrics despite headwinds such as U.S. knee SKU rationalization and a difficult first‑quarter comparison.

Blackman also highlights a compelling longer‑term trajectory, with 2027 expected to benefit from a series of differentiated product launches and a more focused commercial footprint, which he believes can drive a gradual grind higher in earnings off a discounted valuation. Looking beyond 2028, his thesis assumes Zimmer Biomet can sustain mid‑single‑digit revenue growth, expand gross margins toward 70%, and lift EBITDA into the mid‑30% range, turning the business into a strong cash generator, and he maintains his $109 price target unchanged as these elements remain intact.

Blackman covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Intuitive Surgical, and Beta Bionics, Inc.. According to TipRanks, Blackman has an average return of 8.8% and a 44.59% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 13, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $101.00 price target.

data.title: Microsoft Buy Rating Reiterated as Analyst Sees Lower OpenAI Risk, Improved Azure Economics and Keeps $540 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Derrick Wood maintained a Buy rating on Microsoft today and set a price target of $540.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Derrick Wood has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Microsoft’s revised partnership with OpenAI and its financial implications. He views the new agreement as lowering strategic and legal risk while preserving Microsoft’s status as OpenAI’s primary cloud partner for key offerings, and he highlights the long-term license to OpenAI’s IP through 2032 as a foundation for continued AI product innovation on Azure.

He also emphasizes that Microsoft will stop sharing Azure OpenAI Services revenue with OpenAI while still receiving capped revenue-share payments, which he estimates could modestly accelerate Azure growth and enhance profitability. In addition, he notes that Microsoft’s 27% stake in OpenAI may appreciate faster as OpenAI scales on multiple clouds, and he points to reduced capex burden and customer concentration as further support for maintaining a Buy rating and a $540 price target, which remains unchanged.

In another report released today, Scotiabank also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $600.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Zimmer Biomet: Earnings Beat Masks Slowing Growth and Leadership Uncertainty, Supporting Hold Rating
data.description:
Needham analyst Michael Matson has maintained their neutral stance on ZBH stock, giving a Hold rating on April 24.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Michael Matson has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a quarter in which Zimmer Biomet modestly exceeded revenue expectations and delivered an earnings beat that was helped by tariff-related refunds. Management kept its 2026 sales outlook intact while only nudging up the lower bound of earnings guidance, which signals limited incremental upside from current forecasts.

At the same time, the company’s underlying organic revenue growth decelerated, and Matson’s analysis suggests Zimmer Biomet slightly ceded reconstruction market share in a slowing global market. The unexpected departure of the CFO and the appointment of an interim finance head add a layer of uncertainty, and with only low-single-digit top-line expansion and challenges in driving substantial EPS growth, he sees the risk/reward as balanced rather than compelling for new buyers.

In another report released on April 24, Canaccord Genuity also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $93.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Hayes Reiterates Buy on Puig After Q1 Beat, Keeps $20 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
In a report released today, David Hayes from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on Puig Brands, S.A., with a price target of €20.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Hayes has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily Puig’s ability to surpass first-quarter expectations despite a difficult operating environment. The company delivered stronger-than-anticipated organic growth, showing that its core brands and execution remain resilient even as the sector faces macro and channel headwinds.

He also highlights particularly solid momentum in fragrances and a better-than-expected performance in makeup, easing concerns about shifts across sales channels and regional demand, especially in EMEA. While ongoing discussions about a potential tie-up with Estée Lauder provided no fresh details and thus muted the immediate market reaction, he maintains confidence in the equity story and keeps his price target unchanged at $20, supporting the Buy recommendation.

data.title: Analyst Lifts Computacenter PT to 4,400p and Reiterates Buy on Stronger Growth Outlook
data.description:
Analyst Charles Brennan from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on Computacenter and increased the price target to p4,400.00 from p3,700.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Charles Brennan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that strengthen Computacenter’s investment case. He sees the company benefiting from both rising market share and increasing demand tied to large cloud providers’ data‑center investments, which together support higher growth and profitability expectations.

In response to the stronger-than-anticipated recent trading update, he lifted his earnings forecasts for fiscal years 2026 and 2027 by roughly 7–8%. Reflecting this improved outlook and his view of Computacenter as the leading “picks and shovels” operator in its sector, he also increased his price target from 3,700p to 4,400p while reiterating his Buy recommendation.

Brennan covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Computacenter, Temenos, and Bytes Technology Group Plc. According to TipRanks, Brennan has an average return of 8.8% and a 58.90% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Spotify Maintained at Hold as Rising Costs Offset Solid Fundamentals; $525 Price Target Reiterated
data.description:
Spotify, the Communication Services sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Doug Creutz from TD Cowen reiterated a Hold rating on the stock and has a $525.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Doug Creutz has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, balancing solid fundamentals against rising cost pressures. Spotify’s first-quarter revenue, margins, and user metrics were marginally better than expected, with notable improvement in gross margin driven by its Premium segment. However, advertising revenue was soft, and operating income slightly lagged projections when adjusted for one-time items.

More importantly, Creutz highlights that underlying operating expenses are climbing at a faster pace, led by higher marketing outlays as well as growing cloud and AI-related costs. Management’s guidance points to operating income for the rest of the year that falls short of market expectations, limiting near-term earnings upside. As a result, despite healthy free cash flow and unchanged conviction in the long-term story and $525 price target, the risk‑reward profile supports maintaining a Hold rather than moving to a more bullish stance.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on NeuroOne After Reverse Split, Adjusts Valuation and Lowers Price Target to $8
data.description:
Analyst Justin Walsh from JonesTrading reiterated a Buy rating on NeuroOne Medical Technologies and decreased the price target to $8.00 from $9.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Justin Walsh has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including the updated valuation following NeuroOne’s 1-for-6 reverse stock split. After adjusting share counts and projected equity raise prices, his risk-adjusted discounted cash flow model points to a fair market value around $116 million and supports a revised $8 per-share target despite the mechanical nature of the change.

Walsh also highlights the company’s partner-driven strategy for commercializing its RF ablation system, which he believes can underpin a recurring revenue stream and drive a path to profitability. He further sees upside from the pipeline, notably the sEEG-based drug delivery platform and spinal cord percutaneous paddle lead, along with a cash position that management projects will fund operations into fiscal 2026, collectively justifying a continued Buy stance.

According to TipRanks, Walsh is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 20.7% and a 38.69% success rate. Walsh covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Perspective Therapeutics, Beyond Air, and Rapport Therapeutics, Inc..

data.title: CommVault Maintains Buy Rating and $160 Price Target on Strong Q4 Beat and Robust FY27 Outlook
data.description:
Shrenik Kothari, an analyst from Robert W. Baird, maintained the Buy rating on CommVault Systems. The associated price target remains the same with $160.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shrenik Kothari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to CommVault’s strong execution and improved visibility into future growth. The company delivered a clear fourth-quarter beat on both revenue and profitability, with subscription and SaaS metrics accelerating, while new disclosure changes bring reported figures closer to the underlying business reality, increasing investor confidence.

At the same time, management’s FY27 outlook remains conservative on macro and memory dynamics, yet still implies high-teens subscription ARR growth, rising SaaS ARR above $500M, and operating margins above 20%, supporting a favorable risk/reward profile at the current valuation and justifying an unchanged $160 price target. Additional tailwinds from AI-driven data growth, heightened cyber-resilience needs, and expanding SaaS mix, alongside strong net retention and multi-product adoption, further underpin Kothari’s positive stance on the shares.

In another report released on April 16, Guggenheim also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $175.00 price target.
